title: FLS Module 13: Post-Operative Care and Complications

questions.text: Postoperative shoulder pain following laparoscopic surgery typically lasts:
questions.explanation: Referred shoulder pain is due to diaphragmatic irritation from pneumoperitoneum and usually resolves within 1–3 days.

questions.text: Which medication class should not be used to treat postoperative nausea and vomiting (PONV)?
questions.explanation: Opioids contribute to PONV and should be minimized or avoided in its treatment. Other classes are commonly used for prophylaxis and rescue therapy.

questions.text: A patient reports increasing postoperative pain several days after a laparoscopic procedure. The appropriate next step is:

answerOptions.text: A) Prescribe additional opioids without assessment
B) Reassure the patient that it’s normal
C) Schedule an in-person evaluation for possible complications
D) Recommend hot compresses and rest

questions.explanation: Increasing pain after laparoscopy should raise concern for complications such as infection, hematoma, or bowel injury.

questions.text: Which of the following is a well-established risk factor for postoperative nausea and vomiting?
questions.explanation: A history of motion sickness is a strong risk factor. Other strong factors include female sex, non-smoking status, and prior PONV history.

questions.text: If a patient experiences PONV despite prophylaxis with ondansetron, what is the best rescue treatment approach?

answerOptions.text: B) Add morphine for pain control
C) Switch to a different antiemetic class
D) Use metoclopramide only for nausea

questions.explanation: Rescue therapy should use a medication from a different class than that used for prophylaxis to improve efficacy and avoid tolerance.

questions.text: What is the best approach to PONV prevention?

answerOptions.text: B) Use a single antiemetic agent for all patients
C) Risk stratification and tailored multimodal therapy
D) Wait until symptoms arise, then treat

questions.explanation: Individualized treatment based on risk factors and combining different antiemetic classes improves outcomes.

questions.text: Which of the following is not a recommended PONV prophylactic medication?
questions.explanation: Morphine and other opioids increase the risk of PONV and are not used as antiemetics.

questions.text: Which of the following strategies may help reduce postoperative shoulder pain from laparoscopy?

answerOptions.text: C) Evacuating pneumoperitoneum at the end of the case
D) Avoiding use of shoulder supports

questions.explanation: Removing residual gas can reduce diaphragmatic irritation and referred shoulder pain.

questions.text: Postoperative dietary instructions after fundoplication typically include:

answerOptions.text: A) High-fiber diet starting on day 1
C) Return to regular diet within 12 hours

questions.explanation: Temporary dysphagia may occur; a soft diet minimizes discomfort and reduces complication risk.

questions.text: Which of the following laparoscopic procedures has the shortest expected return to normal diet?
questions.explanation: Diet is usually resumed within 0–1 day after laparoscopic appendectomy.

questions.text: Which strategy is most appropriate for postoperative pain management following laparoscopy?

answerOptions.text: D) Elevation of the head of bed

questions.explanation: Combining local anesthetics, non-opioid medications, and minimizing gas insufflation pressure can improve comfort.

questions.text: What is a typical sign of wound complication following laparoscopic surgery?
questions.explanation: Patients should be instructed to watch for signs such as increasing pain, erythema, swelling, or discharge.

questions.text: When can patients usually shower after laparoscopic surgery?
questions.explanation: Most laparoscopic incisions can be exposed to water after 1–2 days, as long as wounds are clean and dry.

questions.text: Which of the following patients is most appropriate for postoperative activity restriction?

answerOptions.text: C) Young adult after laparoscopic appendectomy
D) Patient with no abdominal wall closure issues

questions.explanation: Impaired healing due to immunosuppression warrants a more cautious return to activity.

questions.text: Which technique may improve postoperative pain but has inconsistent evidence?

answerOptions.text: A) Heated and humidified insufflation gas

questions.explanation: Heated and humidified gas may help reduce discomfort, but the evidence supporting this is still mixed.

questions.text: Which factor contributes to delayed return to diet after laparoscopic colon resection?
questions.explanation: Procedures involving extensive bowel resection typically require 3–6 days before diet is fully resumed, largely due to bowel manipulation and recovery.

questions.text: Which discharge practice is recommended to optimize patient safety?

answerOptions.text: B) Discharge without a contact number
C) Written and verbal instructions with physician contact info
D) Generic printouts without procedure specifics

questions.explanation: Clear, personalized written and verbal instructions with a contact number help identify complications early and ensure safe recovery.

questions.text: What postoperative wound dressing is least commonly used in laparoscopy?
questions.explanation: Laparoscopic wounds are small and typically don’t require advanced wound dressings like negative-pressure therapy unless complicated.

questions.text: What is a key strategy in preoperative counseling for laparoscopic patients?

answerOptions.text: C) Discuss operative findings postoperatively only
D) Set realistic expectations for recovery and outcomes

questions.explanation: Educating patients preoperatively about expected recovery and possible complications helps reduce anxiety and improve satisfaction and adherence.

questions.text: Which patients require long-term follow-up after laparoscopic surgery?

answerOptions.text: B) Only those with wound infections
C) Patients treated for morbid obesity or malignancy

questions.explanation: Patients treated for morbid obesity or malignancy often require structured long-term monitoring for weight loss, nutrition, or cancer recurrence.
